hyperedges.label: Medical/Clinical Gaussian Splatting Applications
hyperedges.theme: All three papers apply Gaussian Splatting representations to clinical/medical 3D reconstruction tasks: 0197 for CT tomographic imaging, 0207 for X-ray Digital Subtraction Angiography, and 0209 for dental tooth generation — each extending GS into a specific clinical domain with domain-specific loss functions and data constraints.
source_files: 0197_[General]_Active View Selection with Perturbed Gaussian Ensemble for T.md
0207_[General]_DSA-SRGS Super-Resolution Gaussian Splatting for Dynamic Sp.md
0209_[General]_DM-CFO A Diffusion Model for Compositional 3D Tooth Generat.md

hyperedges.label: Sparse-View and Feed-Forward 3DGS Reconstruction
hyperedges.theme: These papers address the problem of 3DGS reconstruction from limited/sparse input views: 0201 (FTSplat) uses a feed-forward network for single-pass multi-view reconstruction, 0213 uses hierarchical GS with multimodal importance sampling for sparse-view NVS, and 0200 fuses omnidirectional LiDAR+RGB for initialization-efficient 3DGS — all reducing per-scene optimization cost.

hyperedges.label: Physics-Aware and Simulation-Coupled 3DGS
hyperedges.theme: These papers couple 3DGS with physical simulation or physical imaging models: 0205 (GaussTwin) uses PBD rigid body + Cosserat rod physics anchored to Gaussians for robotic digital twins, 0197 uses radiative transport (Beer-Lambert) for CT X-ray Gaussian rendering, and 0207 builds on the same radiative GS model for DSA angiography — all using physical forward models to drive Gaussian optimization.
source_files: 0205_[General]_GaussTwin Unified Simulation and Correction with Gaussian S.md
0197_[General]_Active View Selection with Perturbed Gaussian Ensemble for T.md
0207_[General]_DSA-SRGS Super-Resolution Gaussian Splatting for Dynamic Sp.md
